Reused in the tea tasting cup feature to support discussion of recordable comparison, order, and tasting workflow.
Used in the Chinese and English history feature on tea intangible heritage revival to illustrate embodied hand-processing craft at the center of traditional tea heritage.

Used in the tea intangible heritage feature to show that tea heritage begins in tea landscapes, cultivation knowledge, and tea garden management; also reused in the Huangshan Maofeng feature as a mountain-garden visual supporting discussion of Huizhou highland ecology.
Used in the tea intangible heritage feature to illustrate how traditional tea culture is being translated into contemporary retail and youth-facing tea spaces.
